summary: Fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) require bile salts and micelles for absorption and are stored in liver and adipose tissue.
Fat-soluble vitamins are absorbed without bile salts and excreted in urine.
Fat-soluble vitamins cannot accumulate to toxic levels.
Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness, Bitot's spots, and xerophthalmia.
Vitamin A deficiency causes rickets and osteomalacia.
Vitamin A deficiency causes scurvy and bleeding gums.
The three active forms of Vitamin A are retinol, retinal, and retinoic acid.
The three forms of Vitamin A are calciferol, calcitriol, and calcidiol.
Vitamin A has only one active form: retinol.
Vitamin D deficiency causes rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults, affecting 70-90% of Indians.
Vitamin D deficiency causes pellagra in children and beriberi in adults.
Vitamin D deficiency is rare in India due to abundant sunlight.
Vitamin K is essential for synthesis of clotting factors II, VII, IX, and X.
Vitamin K is essential for synthesis of factors I, V, VIII, and XIII.
Vitamin K is required for platelet production only.
Water-soluble vitamins are excreted in urine with low toxicity risk, except B12 which is stored in the liver for 3-5 years.
All water-soluble vitamins are stored for years in the liver.
Water-soluble vitamins have high toxicity risk due to rapid accumulation.
Vitamin C deficiency causes scurvy with bleeding gums, poor wound healing, and easy bruising.
Vitamin C deficiency causes night blindness and dry skin.
Vitamin C deficiency causes peripheral neuropathy and glossitis.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review vitamin classification and deficiency diseases.
Good effort! Revise the fat-soluble and water-soluble vitamin properties.
Excellent understanding of vitamins and nutrition!
